skill.name: nature-response
skill.description: Draft, audit, and revise point-by-point reviewer response letters for Nature-family and other scientific journal revisions. It helps preserve reviewer comments, map responses to manuscript changes, and flag missing author input.
skill.summary: Prepare traceable reviewer response letters for scientific manuscript revisions.
skill.author: Community contribution, refactored into static/dynamic layers

security_audit.summary: All static findings were evaluated as false positives after reviewing the skill context. The package is documentation-only Markdown and YAML for academic reviewer-response drafting, with no executable code, shell commands, network calls, or prompt-injection text. No evidence found of data exfiltration, host reconnaissance, malware behavior, or hidden operational instructions.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backtick Formatting
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged 341 Ruby/shell backtick execution patterns, but the reviewed files are Markdown and YAML documentation. The flagged backticks mark labels, paths, example fields, and fenced text blocks; they are not executable shell or Ruby code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The representative locations contain prose, inline code spans, and fenced example text only. No scripts, command APIs, or executable source files were found in the skill.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keywords
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak-cryptography alerts occur in documentation and review-response guidance, not in code that hashes, encrypts, signs, or validates data. No cryptographic function calls or security-sensitive algorithms are present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files are human-readable Markdown or YAML instructions for academic writing. Searches found no md5, sha1, encryption, signing, or password-handling implementation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Terms in Academic Context
low_findings.description: System and network reconnaissance alerts are caused by ordinary academic review language and source references. The skill does not inspect the host system, enumerate networks, or request environment details.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are reviewer-response examples, checklists, and source citations. No commands or network-scanning instructions are present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Hardcoded URLs Are Citations
low_findings.description: Five hardcoded URL findings in references/source-basis.md are journal and publisher citations used to ground writing guidance. The skill contains no fetch, curl, wget, or other network request logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear in a Markdown source table and are never invoked by code. Repository search found no network client API usage in the skill files.

low_findings.title: False Positive: README Entropy Heuristic
low_findings.description: README.md was flagged for high entropy, but the file is normal Chinese Markdown documentation with headings, lists, paths, and a file tree. No encoded, binary, encrypted, or obfuscated payload was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows readable documentation from start to finish. The entropy signal is consistent with mixed-language Markdown and structured file paths.

content.value_statement: Reviewer-response letters demand complete, traceable answers under deadline pressure. This skill structures each comment, response, risk flag, and missing author input.
actual_capabilities: Drafts point-by-point responses for major, minor, and revise-and-resubmit decisions.
Extracts editor instructions and reviewer comments into stable IDs such as E.1 and R1.1.
Classifies comments by severity, category, proposed action, readiness, and missing input.
Maps claimed changes to manuscript locations, figures, tables, citations, or visible placeholders.
Audits draft responses for missing comments, unsupported claims, tone problems, and unresolved risks.
Supports Chinese author notes with English response drafting and Chinese confirmation blocks.
limitations: It cannot invent experiments, statistics, citations, line numbers, or manuscript changes.
It cannot guarantee acceptance by editors or reviewers.
It needs the user to provide real reviewer comments and revision facts.
It does not draft a cover letter or appeal letter by default.

use_cases.title: Major Revision Package
use_cases.description: Turn a long decision letter into a strategy summary, tracker, response draft, and missing-information list.

use_cases.title: Final Response Audit
use_cases.description: Check an existing response letter against reviewer comments before journal resubmission.
use_cases.target_user: Research teams preparing a final revision package

use_cases.title: Chinese Author Notes
use_cases.description: Convert Chinese revision notes into professional English responses while preserving questions for the author.
use_cases.target_user: Chinese-speaking researchers submitting to international journals

prompt_templates.title: Draft From Comments
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a first response draft.
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft a point-by-point response letter from these reviewer comments. Use placeholders where manuscript locations or results are missing: [paste comments].

prompt_templates.title: Revise My Draft
prompt_templates.scenario: Improve tone and completeness in an existing draft.
prompt_templates.prompt: Revise this response draft for clarity, tone, and completeness. Flag every claim that needs a manuscript location: [paste draft].

prompt_templates.title: Resolve Conflicting Reviewers
prompt_templates.scenario: Handle reviewer requests that point in different directions.
prompt_templates.prompt: Reviewer 1 asks for [request]. Reviewer 2 asks for [conflicting request]. Build a response strategy and draft diplomatic replies.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Before Submission
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced QA pass against the full revision package.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this response letter against the editor letter, reviewer comments, and revision notes. Return unresolved risks and required author input: [paste materials].

output_examples.input: Reviewer asks for a larger cohort, but the author only added a power analysis.
output_examples.output: The skill would acknowledge the concern, explain the supplied analysis, soften unsupported claims, and mark any missing cohort details as author input.

output_examples.input: Reviewer comments are in English, and the author notes are in Chinese.
output_examples.output: The skill would draft English point-by-point replies and add a short Chinese confirmation list for unresolved facts.

output_examples.input: Two reviewers request opposite changes to the Discussion section.
output_examples.output: The skill would identify the conflict, preserve both concerns, propose a balanced revision, and avoid promising incompatible changes.

best_practices: Provide the editor letter, reviewer comments, revision notes, and available manuscript locations together.
Keep placeholders visible until the author supplies exact experiments, citations, figures, or line numbers.
Run a final audit after drafting to catch missing comments, unsupported claims, and tone issues.
anti_patterns: Do not ask the skill to invent results, p-values, citations, figures, or reviewer identities.
Do not collapse multiple reviewer concerns into one vague response.
Do not use the ordinary revision workflow for appeals without explicitly routing the case as appeal-like.

faq.question: Which journals does this skill support?
faq.answer: It is designed for Nature-family revisions, but it can help with point-by-point responses for many scientific journals.

faq.question: Can it write a complete response letter?
faq.answer: Yes, when the user provides reviewer comments and enough real revision facts to support each response.

faq.question: Can it work with Chinese notes?
faq.answer: Yes. It can use Chinese author notes and produce English response text with Chinese confirmation items.

faq.question: Will it invent missing manuscript details?
faq.answer: No. The skill is designed to mark missing facts as author input instead of fabricating details.

faq.question: Does it handle disagreements with reviewers?
faq.answer: Yes. It suggests respectful, evidence-based disagreement language and flags when the claim needs support.

faq.question: Does it guarantee acceptance?
faq.answer: No. It improves response structure and traceability, but journal decisions depend on the science and editorial review.
